items.title: Threat Brief: Exploitation of PAN-OS Captive Portal Zero-Day for Unauthenticated Remote Code Execution
items.description: A buffer overflow vulnerability in the User-ID Authentication Portal of PAN-OS software allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls. Limited exploitation has been observed starting April 9, 2026, by a likely state-sponsored threat cluster. Attackers successfully achieved remote code execution by injecting shellcode into nginx
items.ai_summary: The exploitation of a zero-day buffer overflow vulnerability in the User-ID Authentication Portal of PAN-OS software allows unauthenticated attackers to execute arbitrary code with root privileges on PA-Series and VM-Series firewalls. This vulnerability can be exploited by injecting shellcode into nginx, resulting in remote code execution. The real-world impact of this vulnerability is significant, as it can allow attackers to gain control of the firewall and potentially access the entire network. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately apply the latest security patch to their PAN-OS software, ensuring they are running a version that is not vulnerable to this exploit. Additionally, defenders should monitor their firewalls for suspicious activity and implement a web application firewall to detect and prevent similar attacks.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1190| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1203| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Operation GriefLure: Dissecting an APT Campaign Targeting Vietnam's Military Telecom & Philippine Healthcare
items.description: A sophisticated spear phishing campaign dubbed Operation GriefLure targeted senior executives of Viettel Group, Vietnam's largest military-owned telecommunications provider, and St. Luke's Medical Center in the Philippines. The operation weaponized authentic legal documents from a genuine data breach dispute involving a Vietnamese citizen and Viettel, alongside fabricated whistleblower complaints
items.ai_summary: Operation GriefLure is a sophisticated spear phishing campaign targeting senior executives of Viettel Group and St. Luke's Medical Center, utilizing authentic legal documents and fabricated whistleblower complaints to deceive victims. The attack vector involves phishing emails, classified as T1566, which are designed to trick users into executing malicious files, categorized as T1204. If exploited, this campaign could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information, potentially affecting national security and patient data. To remediate, defenders should implement email filters to detect and block phishing attempts, and educate users on how to identify and report suspicious emails. Additionally, organizations should conduct regular security audits to ensure the integrity of their systems and data.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1082| E[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: AI-Assisted Lure Factory Targets Developers & Gamers
items.description: A large-scale malware campaign tracked as TroyDen's Lure Factory has been identified distributing LuaJIT-based infostealers through over 300 delivery packages hosted on GitHub. The operation uses AI-generated lure names incorporating obscure biological taxonomy and medical terminology to target developers, gamers, Roblox players, and crypto users. The malware employs a two-component design with a
items.ai_summary: The TroyDen's Lure Factory campaign is a large-scale malware operation that targets developers, gamers, Roblox players, and crypto users by distributing LuaJIT-based infostealers through over 300 delivery packages hosted on GitHub. The malware uses AI-generated lure names that incorporate obscure biological taxonomy and medical terminology, making it difficult for victims to identify the malicious files. The attack vector involves user execution (T1204) where the victim unknowingly executes the malware, which then employs a two-component design to steal sensitive information. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement strict access controls on GitHub repositories, monitor for suspicious activity related to LuaJIT-based executables, and educate users about the risks of executing unknown files. Additionally, defenders can use network traffic monitoring tools to detect and block communication between the malware and its command and control servers.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Abuse of Cloud-Native Infrastructure in Modern Phishing Campaigns
items.description: An investigation has revealed a structural evolution in phishing operations where threat actors conduct entire campaigns through legitimate, enterprise-trusted cloud infrastructure rather than attacker-controlled systems. Adversaries weaponize platforms employees use daily, including cloud storage, productivity suites, and OAuth authentication endpoints. Attacks originate from legitimate Google or
items.ai_summary: The threat actors are leveraging cloud-native infrastructure to conduct phishing campaigns, utilizing legitimate cloud storage, productivity suites, and OAuth authentication endpoints to trick employees into divulging sensitive information. This attack vector is particularly concerning as it originates from trusted sources, such as Google, making it more challenging for defenders to detect and prevent. The real-world impact of this campaign is significant, as it can lead to unauthorized access to sensitive data, financial loss, and reputational damage. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement specific security controls, such as multi-factor authentication, conditional access policies, and regular monitoring of cloud infrastructure for suspicious activity. Additionally, organizations should educate their employees on the risks of phishing attacks and provide training on how to identify and report suspicious emails or activities.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR

    B -->|T1625| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Execution]:::tactic
    D -->|T1557| E[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Agentic AI Uncovers New China-Linked Cluster OP-512
items.description: A newly identified China-linked espionage cluster designated OP-512 has been discovered targeting Internet Information Services (IIS) servers through advanced AI-driven detection. The operation involves deploying a sophisticated custom web shell framework consisting of three components: a file manager with command-and-control notification channel and two cryptographically authenticated command han
items.ai_summary: The OP-512 cluster, linked to China, has been identified as a threat to Internet Information Services (IIS) servers, leveraging advanced AI-driven detection to deploy a custom web shell framework. This framework consists of three components, including a file manager and two cryptographically authenticated command handlers, facilitating command-and-control operations. The attack vector involves exploiting server software components, as indicated by TTP T1505, and utilizing application layer protocols, as seen in T1071. To mitigate this threat, defenders should implement specific security measures, such as configuring IIS servers to restrict access to sensitive directories and updating server software to the latest versions. Additionally, monitoring network traffic for suspicious application layer protocol activity can help detect potential OP-512 cluster operations.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1505| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Threat Brief: Active Exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257
items.description: An unidentified threat actor is actively exploiting CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass vulnerability in PAN-OS GlobalProtect portal and gateway components. The flaw allows unauthorized attackers to circumvent security controls and initiate VPN connections. The vulnerability was added to CISA's Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog on May 29, 2026. Exploitation activity has been detected tar
items.ai_summary: The active exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257, an authentication bypass vulnerability in PAN-OS GlobalProtect portal and gateway components, allows unauthorized attackers to circumvent security controls and initiate VPN connections. This vulnerability affects PAN-OS versions prior to the patched version, which has not been specified. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to unauthorized access to the network, potentially resulting in data breaches or lateral movement. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should immediately apply the patch for CVE-2026-0257 and ensure that all GlobalProtect portal and gateway components are updated to the latest version. Additionally, defenders should monitor their networks for suspicious activity and implement additional security controls, such as multi-factor authentication, to prevent exploitation.

items.title: Seeking Counsel: Ongoing Targeted Campaign Against US Law Firms
items.description: From January through May 2026, a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign executed by threat cluster UNC3753 targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the United States. The threat actors leverage voice phishing and social engineering techniques, posing as IT support to convince targets to host screen-sharing sessions and download remote moni
items.ai_summary: The UNC3753 threat cluster has been conducting a targeted campaign against US law firms from January to May 2026, utilizing voice phishing and social engineering tactics to trick victims into hosting screen-sharing sessions and downloading remote monitoring tools. The attackers pose as IT support personnel to gain the trust of their targets, leveraging T1598.004 spearphishing voice techniques and T1566 phishing to initiate the attack. Once access is gained, the threat actors use T1204 user execution to execute malicious code, followed by T1041 exfiltration over a C2 channel to steal sensitive data. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement multi-factor authentication, conduct regular security awareness training, and monitor for unusual network activity, specifically focusing on inbound and outbound traffic on non-standard ports. Additionally, law firms should verify the identity of anyone claiming to be IT support before allowing remote access to their systems.

graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1598.004| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1566| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Privilege Escalation]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: VerdantBamboo: Just Another BRICKSTORM in the Firewall
items.description: Chinese threat actor VerdantBamboo compromised a victim organization and its Managed Services Provider over an 18-month period, deploying malware on network edge devices lacking EDR coverage. The initial breach involved an Egnyte Storage Sync system, where attackers exploited a sudo misconfiguration for privilege escalation and installed BRICKSTORM backdoor and AGENTPSD fallback implant. Investiga
items.ai_summary: The VerdantBamboo threat actor exploited a sudo misconfiguration on an Egnyte Storage Sync system to gain initial access, leveraging T1068 for privilege escalation. This allowed them to install the BRICKSTORM backdoor and AGENTPSD fallback implant on network edge devices, which lacked Endpoint Detection and Response (EDR) coverage. The real-world impact includes the compromise of a victim organization and its Managed Services Provider over an 18-month period, resulting in potential data breaches and unauthorized access. To remediate, defenders should ensure proper configuration of sudo privileges, implement EDR solutions on all network devices, and monitor for suspicious activity related to T1649 and T1219. Specifically, defenders should review their Egnyte Storage Sync configurations and update to the latest version, while also enforcing multi-factor authentication and regularly auditing authentication certificates.

    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Miasma Worm Campaign Spreads with New PyPI Wave
items.description: A coordinated PyPI compromise campaign involving 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 packages was detected, utilizing Python startup hooks to execute credential-stealing payloads. The attack leverages .pth files for automatic execution during Python interpreter startup, downloads the Bun JavaScript runtime, and runs obfuscated JavaScript payloads. The malware targets high-value developer and CI
items.ai_summary: The Miasma Worm Campaign is a coordinated PyPI compromise campaign that involves 37 malicious wheel artifacts across 19 packages, utilizing Python startup hooks to execute credential-stealing payloads. The attack leverages .pth files for automatic execution during Python interpreter startup, downloads the Bun JavaScript runtime, and runs obfuscated JavaScript payloads. This campaign targets high-value developer and CI environments, potentially leading to significant data breaches and unauthorized access. To remediate, defenders should immediately inspect their Python environments for suspicious .pth files and remove any malicious artifacts, then update their package dependencies to the latest versions and implement strict access controls for CI environments. Additionally, monitoring for unusual JavaScript runtime downloads and executions can help detect potential infections.

graph LR

    B -->|T1027| C[Defense Evasion]:::tactic
    C -->|T1550| D[Credential Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1041| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Technical Advisory: Breach of Instructure Canvas LMS
items.description: In early May 2026, Instructure confirmed a breach affecting its Canvas learning platform after detecting unauthorized activity on May 1. ShinyHunters exploited the Free-For-Teacher account program, compromising the Canvas platform directly and exposing names, email addresses, student IDs, and private messages. The exposure window ran from April 30 to May 7, 2026. ShinyHunters claims 3.6 TB of data
items.ai_summary: The breach of Instructure Canvas LMS, a popular learning management system, occurred between April 30 and May 7, 2026, when threat actors exploited the Free-For-Teacher account program to gain unauthorized access to the platform. This breach, attributed to the ShinyHunters group, exposed sensitive information including names, email addresses, student IDs, and private messages, with the attackers claiming to have obtained 3.6 TB of data. The attack vector involved exploiting the free account program, highlighting the need for robust access controls and monitoring. To remediate, Instructure Canvas LMS administrators should immediately review and update their access controls, ensuring that all accounts, especially those created under the Free-For-Teacher program, are thoroughly vetted and monitored for suspicious activity. Furthermore, updating to the latest version of the Canvas platform and ensuring all security patches are applied is crucial, although specific version numbers are not provided due to the nature of the breach being more related to the exploitation of a program rather than a specific software vulnerability.

graph LR

    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1204| D[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-44238: FreePBX is an open source IP PBX. Prior to 16.0.50 and 17.0.11, the CDR Reports ...
items.description: FreePBX is an open source IP PBX. Prior to 16.0.50 and 17.0.11, the CDR Reports module page allows SQL injection through the order and sort POST parameters. Authentication with a FreePBX Administration Control Panel account that has CDR section access is required. Full administrator privileges are not needed. This vulnerability is fixed in 16.0.50 and 17.0.11.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-44238 is a SQL injection vulnerability in the CDR Reports module of FreePBX, an open-source IP PBX, affecting versions prior to 16.0.50 and 17.0.11. The vulnerability can be exploited through the order and sort POST parameters on the CDR Reports module page, requiring authentication with a FreePBX Administration Control Panel account that has CDR section access. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow an attacker to inject malicious SQL code, potentially leading to unauthorized data access or modification. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update their FreePBX installations to version 16.0.50 or 17.0.11, and restrict access to the CDR Reports module to only necessary personnel. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity, such as unusual database queries or login attempts from unfamiliar locations.

items.title: CVE-2026-44237: FreePBX is an open source IP PBX. Prior to 17.0.8, the FreePBX api module's OAut...
items.description: FreePBX is an open source IP PBX. Prior to 17.0.8, the FreePBX api module's OAuth2 implementation does not sufficiently validate client credentials during token issuance. Knowledge of a valid client_id is required. The validateClient() method in ClientRepository.php unconditionally returns true, allowing any party with knowledge of a valid client_id to obtain OAuth2 access tokens without providing
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-44237 is a vulnerability in the FreePBX API module's OAuth2 implementation, affecting versions prior to 17.0.8. The vulnerability allows an attacker with knowledge of a valid client_id to obtain OAuth2 access tokens without providing valid credentials, due to the validateClient() method in ClientRepository.php unconditionally returning true. This can lead to unauthorized access to the FreePBX system, potentially allowing attackers to make unauthorized calls, access sensitive information, or disrupt service. To remediate, defenders should update to FreePBX version 17.0.8 or later and ensure that all client credentials are properly validated. Additionally, defenders should monitor their systems for suspicious activity and consider implementing additional security measures, such as multi-factor authentication and IP whitelisting.

items.title: CVE-2026-40528: OpenSC before 0.27.0, fixed in commit 0358817, contains a stack and heap buffer ...
items.description: OpenSC before 0.27.0, fixed in commit 0358817, contains a stack and heap buffer overrun vulnerability in the do_key_value() function in src/pkcs15init/profile.c that allows attackers to corrupt memory by supplying a crafted profile configuration file. During pkcs15-init invocation, a key value entry beginning with '=' followed by more than sizeof(keybuf) characters is copied into keybuf via memcpy
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40528 is a stack and heap buffer overrun vulnerability in OpenSC before version 0.27.0, specifically in the do_key_value() function in src/pkcs15init/profile.c. This vulnerability can be exploited by attackers who supply a crafted profile configuration file, allowing them to corrupt memory. The attack vector involves a key value entry beginning with '=' followed by more than sizeof(keybuf) characters, which is then copied into keybuf via memcpy during pkcs15-init invocation. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update OpenSC to version 0.27.0 or later, and ensure that all profile configuration files are validated and sanitized to prevent malicious input. Additionally, defenders can apply the patch from commit 0358817 to fix the vulnerability.

items.title: CVE-2026-40510: OpenSC before 0.27.0-rc1, fixed in commit 3f24f0b, contains a stack buffer overf...
items.description: OpenSC before 0.27.0-rc1, fixed in commit 3f24f0b, contains a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in piv_process_history() in src/libopensc/card-piv.c that allows physically present attackers to trigger memory corruption by presenting a crafted PIV smart card or USB device returning a URL field longer than 118 bytes in the Key History Object ASN.1 response.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-40510 is a stack buffer overflow vulnerability in OpenSC before version 0.27.0-rc1, specifically in the piv_process_history() function in src/libopensc/card-piv.c. This vulnerability can be exploited by physically present attackers who present a crafted PIV smart card or USB device with a URL field longer than 118 bytes in the Key History Object ASN.1 response, leading to memory corruption. If exploited, this vulnerability could allow attackers to execute arbitrary code on the affected system, potentially leading to data breaches or other malicious activities. To remediate this vulnerability, defenders should update OpenSC to version 0.27.0-rc1 or later, and ensure that all dependent applications are recompiled against the updated library. Additionally, defenders can mitigate this vulnerability by restricting access to PIV smart cards and USB devices, and implementing strict input validation on all external data.

items.description: DreamMaker developed by Interinfo has a Path Traversal vulnerability, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to read file names under arbitrary path by exploiting an Absolute Path Traversal vulnerability.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-10075 is a Path Traversal vulnerability in DreamMaker developed by Interinfo, allowing unauthenticated remote attackers to read file names under arbitrary paths. The vulnerability can be exploited by sending a crafted request to the application, potentially leading to sensitive information disclosure. The CVSS score of 5.3 indicates a moderate severity level. To remediate this vulnerability, users should update DreamMaker to the latest version and restrict access to sensitive files and directories. Additionally, implementing proper input validation and sanitization can help prevent similar vulnerabilities in the future.

Every polished message adds another case for Tier 1 to review, another link to inspect, and another alert that cannot be dismissed at a glance. These aren't "whoops, this line right here is wrong, and that's RCE." They're novel combinations of a few dozen issues out of thousands of things every SAST scanner already finds, chained together into something much worse. It's real creativity,

items.description: Focusing on hacking law firms in the US, the ransomware group relies on fast flux to hide its C&C infrastructure. The post Silent Ransom Group Uses DNS Fast Flux in Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: VerdantBamboo Deploys BSD Variant of BRICKSTORM on Linux Appliances
items.description: A China-nexus cyber espionage group has been observed deploying a BSD variant of a known backdoor called BRICKSTORM, as well as two other malware families codenamed PLENET (aka GRIMBOLT) and AGENTPSD to target Linux systems. The activity has been attributed by Volexity to a threat cluster it tracks as VerdantBamboo, which it said overlaps with hacking groups known as Clay Typhoon (Microsoft),

items.title: OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls
items.description: The Active Sessions and Lockdown Mode features are being made more broadly available by the AI giant. The post OpenAI Rolling Out ChatGPT Account Security Controls appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Anthropic Urges Industry Coordination to Allow for a ‘Pause’ in AI Development if Risks Grow
items.description: The proposed coordination would let advanced AI labs verify that global rivals have actually stopped or slowed their work. The post Anthropic Urges Industry Coordination to Allow for a ‘Pause’ in AI Development if Risks Grow appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: SolarWinds Serv-U Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild
items.description: Unauthenticated attackers can exploit the flaw via specially crafted POST requests that crash the Serv-U service. The post SolarWinds Serv-U Vulnerability Exploited in the Wild appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: UNC3753 Used Vishing and Physical Intrusions in U.S. Data Theft Extortion Campaign
items.description: Cybersecurity researchers have disclosed details of a financially motivated data theft extortion campaign that has targeted dozens of organizations across professional, legal, and financial services in the U.S. between January and May 2026. The activity has been attributed by Google Mandiant and Google Threat Intelligence Group (GTIG) to a threat actor dubbed UNC3753, which is also known as

items.title: Meta Says 20,000 Instagram Accounts Hacked via AI Tool Abuse
items.description: The social media giant has informed authorities about the impact of the recent attack involving an account recovery support tool. The post Meta Says 20,000 Instagram Accounts Hacked via AI Tool Abuse appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: VS Code Adds 2-Hour Extension Auto-Update Delay to Limit Supply Chain Attacks
items.description: Microsoft has announced that Visual Studio Code (VS Code) will apply a two-hour delay before extensions for the integrated development environment (IDE) are updated automatically to a newer version in an attempt to tackle software supply chain threats. "When automatic updates are enabled, new versions are auto-updated two hours after they are published, adding an extra layer of protection

items.title: Over 20,000 Instagram accounts stolen in Meta AI support hack
items.description: Meta has revealed that 20,225 Instagram users had their accounts hijacked in a recent incident where attackers used Meta's AI-powered support system to reset passwords. [...]

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 8th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9962, (Mon, Jun 8th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Hands on with Intelligent Terminal, an AI-powered Windows Terminal
items.description: Microsoft has created an open-source fork of Windows Terminal called "Intelligent Terminal," and it allows you to use AI directly inside Terminal without interfering with the regular session. [...]

items.title: C0XMO botnet spreads via DD-WRT router flaw, kills rival malware
items.description: A new variant of the Gafgyt botnet called C0XMO is targeting DD-WRT router firmware and can move to other device types with various CPU architectures. [...]

items.title: Silent Ransom Group targets law firms with fake IT support calls
items.description: The Silent Ransom Group extortion gang is actively targeting U.S. law firms and professional services organizations in social engineering attacks that often lead to data theft within hours of initial contact, according to a new report by cybersecurity firm Mandiant. [...]

items.title: Emphere Raises $2.1 Million for AI-Powered Vulnerability Remediation
items.description: Emphere’s solution delivers AI-driven remediation to software companies to speed up releases. The post Emphere Raises $2.1 Million for AI-Powered Vulnerability Remediation appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Critical Everest Forms Pro flaw exploited to take over WordPress sites
items.description: Hackers are actively exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-3300) in the Everest Forms Pro plugin, which lets them take complete control of a WordPress website. [...]

items.title: New ChatGPT Lockdown Mode Limits Tools That Could Enable Data Exfiltration
items.description: OpenAI has begun rolling out a new Lockdown Mode to ChatGPT for eligible personal accounts to reduce the risk of data exfiltration arising from prompt injection attacks. The feature is primarily designed for people and organizations that handle sensitive data and require stricter protection guarantees. Lockdown Mode is available to logged-in users across Free, Go, Plus, and Pro, and

items.title: Free Apps Are Quietly Turning Smart TVs Into Web-Scraping Proxies for AI
items.description: A researcher has reverse-engineered the iOS SDK that Bright Data embeds in consumer apps and documented how it turns devices, including always-on smart TVs, into exit nodes that relay web-scraping traffic for a data business Bright Data markets heavily to the AI industry. The company, the successor to Luminati, operates what it calls the largest residential proxy network in the world,

items.title: CISA Adds Actively Exploited SolarWinds Serv-U DoS Flaw to KEV Catalog
items.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) has added a high-severity security flaw impacting SolarWinds Serv-U multi-protocol file server software to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-28318 (CVSS score: 7.5), is a denial-of-service (DoS) bug that causes the service to crash

items.title: AI Agent Uncovers 21 Zero-Days in FFmpeg; Chrome Patches Record 429 Bugs
items.description: Two things landed within days of each other this week. A security startup reported 21 previously unknown vulnerabilities in FFmpeg, the media library inside almost everything that touches video, all of them found by an autonomous AI agent. The same week, Google shipped Chrome 149 with patches for 429 security bugs, the most ever in a single release. Only the FFmpeg bugs were found by AI.

items.title: Miasma Worm Hits 73 Microsoft GitHub Repositories in Major Supply Chain Attack
items.description: Microsoft's GitHub repositories have become the latest to fall victim to the ongoing Miasma self-replicating supply chain attack campaign. The incident impacted 73 Microsoft repositories across four of its GitHub organizations, including Azure, Azure-Samples, Microsoft, and MicrosoftDocs, per OpenSourceMalware. The development has GitHub to disable access to those repositories. "Access to this

items.title: Suspicious Polyfill login prompts pop up on Toshiba, Muji websites
items.description: Tech giant Toshiba and mega-retailer Muji warned visitors that suspicious sign-in screens popping up on their websites could collect credentials. [...]

items.title: CISA: Hackers now exploit SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers
items.description: CISA warned today that hackers are now actively exploiting a recently patched high-severity SolarWinds Serv-U flaw to crash servers. [...]

items.title: Exposed Fuel Tank Gauges Under Attack in the US
items.description: Threat actors are taking advantage of Internet-exposed tank gauges by breaching gas stations, opening the door to disruption.

items.title: Chinese APT deploys new malware to keep access to hacked networks
items.description: A Chinese espionage group tracked as UNC5221 has been accessing Microsoft 365 environments using the Brickstorm backdoor and previously undocumented malware named Plenet and AgentPSD. [...]

items.title: Adaptive, Agentic AI Worms Loom as Next Enterprise Threat
items.description: AI worms, or "viruses with wings and brains," adapt to new environments, seek out vulnerabilities, and will likely strike within a year, researchers say.

items.title: EU unveils tech sovereignty package to cut reliance on US, Chinese suppliers
items.description: The package bundles two draft laws — a Chips Act 2.0 and a Cloud and AI Development Act (CADA) — alongside an Open Source Strategy and a roadmap for digitalizing the energy system.

items.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
items.description: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Friday, June 5th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9960, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Apple removes Russia’s state-backed messaging app Max from its store
items.description: Russian authorities have promoted Max as a domestic alternative to foreign messaging platforms such as Telegram and WhatsApp.

items.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
items.description: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.

items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.description: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
items.description: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.

items.title: Trump considers Palantir exec to lead CISA
items.description: Shyam Sankar, the chief technology officer at Palantir Technologies, has emerged as a lead contender for the long vacant Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) director role, according to the sources, who requested anonymity to discuss the administration’s search.

items.title: FTC considers setting aside or modifying $150 million privacy penalty against X
items.description: Twitter, renamed X in 2023, filed a petition saying that the settlement terms are unfair because the order was issued against a company that “no longer exists,” the workers responsible for the scheme no longer work for X and the firm has since established a “world class” privacy and data protection program.

items.title: Russia seeks to label two anti-Kremlin hacker groups as ‘extremist’
items.description: The groups have previously claimed responsibility for cyberattacks targeting critical infrastructure and government institutions in Russia and Belarus.

items.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
items.description: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: Microsoft's Coreutils for Windows, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: I&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;ve been using the GnuWin32 CoreUtils for Windows for many years now (it gives you many *nix core commands on Windows).

items.title: Pakistan Spies on Afghan Finance Ministry With Xeno RAT
items.description: Despite broadly connected digital infrastructure, standard fare TTPs are enough to cause trouble for Afghanistan's porous cybersecurity.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, June 4th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9958, (Thu, Jun 4th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Attackers Use AI to Automate EDR Evasion Testing
items.description: Python scripts were used to test malware against endpoint detection and response agents from Sophos, CrowdStrike, and Windows Defender.

items.title: Tropical Blend: Cyber & Politics Ramp Up Across Latin America
items.description: China-linked espionage groups have attacked at least a dozen nations in the region, gathering information on maritime shipping, oil production, and other geopolitical interests.

items.title: Continuing Scans for swagger.json, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: Enterprise applications often still use complex standards like SOAP for web services. The big advantage of SOAP is its tight and extensive standards, which enable interoperability across an enterprise governed by web services. The disadvantage of SOAP: First, while it is de facto usually used over HTTP, it does not leverage HTTP, leading to unnecessary complexity. Secondly, kids don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t RTFM, and developers these days tend not to appreciate the art of careful system design; they rather throw code at an IDE to see what sticks, if they don&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t vibe code it anyway.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Wednesday, June 3rd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9956, (Wed, Jun 3rd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Urges Stronger Security for Automatic Tank Gauge Systems
New Wave Of Phishing Emails with SVG Files, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: For a few days, my SANS ISC mailbox is flooded with emails that delivers SVG files. An SVG ("Scalable Vector Graphic") is a web-friendly vector file format used for graphics and icons. No URL in the body, just â€œan imageâ€, that&#x27s the perfect way to deliver some malicious content. This isn&#x27t the first time that we see this technique used by threat actors[1].

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Tuesday, June 2nd, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9954, (Tue, Jun 2nd)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
items.description: The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Monday, June 1st, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9952, (Mon, Jun 1st)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA Announces Revised Town Hall Schedule to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
items.description: Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequent staging ground for cyber mischief from Russia's intelligence agencies.

items.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
items.description: Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.

items.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster ‘Dort’ Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
items.description: Canadian authorities on Wednesday arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man on suspicion of building and operating Kimwolf, a fast spreading Internet-of-Things botnet that enslaved millions of devices for use in a series of massive distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks over the past six months. KrebsOnSecurity publicly named the suspect in February 2026 after the accused launched a volley of DDoS, doxing and swatting campaigns against this author and a security researcher. He now faces criminal hacking charges in both Canada and the United States.

items.title: CISA Enhances Known Exploited Vulnerabilities Catalog to Include New Nomination Form
CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
items.description: Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
items.description: Artificial intelligence platforms may be just as susceptible to social engineering as human beings, but they are proving remarkably good at finding security vulnerabilities in human-made computer code. That reality is on full display this month with some of the more widely-used software makers -- including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla and Oracle -- fixing near record volumes of security bugs, and/or quickening the tempo of their patch releases.

items.title: Canvas Breach Disrupts Schools & Colleges Nationwide
items.description: An ongoing data extortion attack targeting the widely-used education technology platform Canvas disrupted classes and coursework at school districts and universities across the United States today, after a cybercrime group defaced the service's login page with a ransom demand that threatened to leak data from 275 million students and faculty across nearly 9,000 educational institutions.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
